paper: A Survey for LLM Agent Trajectory Analysis: From Failure Attribution to Enhancement
authors: Wang, Wang, Chen, Xie, Chen, Mu, Liu, Wang
idea: Surveys 42 papers (from 1452 screened) organizing trajectory analysis into failure attribution, debugging, repair, optimization, monitoring, and benchmarks.
gap: Read this first - defines the existing map of the exact space you're entering.

idea: Online prefix-only auditing that alarms at the earliest decisive error in a trajectory; 7B auditor beats GPT-4.1/DeepSeek-V4-Pro on step localization.
gap: External judge model reads text trajectory, not the acting agent's own internal state - your differentiation point.

paper: TrajAD: Trajectory Anomaly Detection for Trustworthy LLM Agents
authors: Liu, Zhang, Han, Liu, Wang, Yu, Wang, Yin
idea: Anomaly detection framework for agent trajectories aimed at trustworthiness.
gap: Check whether it uses internal model signals or only external/behavioral features.

idea: Post-hoc failure diagnosis via failure-keyword folding + investigator agent for coding agents.

paper: Beyond Resolution Rates: Behavioral Drivers of Coding Agent Success and Failure
idea: Structural trajectory sequencing (read/patch/validate) distinguishes success/failure better than trajectory length.
gap: Calls for real-time monitoring but doesn't build it.

idea: Names a failure mode: agent reaches correct code then overwrites/thrashes it; length-independent.
gap: Doesn't test whether internal confidence dips at the collapse point.

idea: Survey of coding agent architectures; failure trajectories run 12-82% longer than successful ones.
Diagnostic benchmark that injects controlled perturbations into web environments to test agent robustness, rather than post-hoc analysis.
gap: Interventional, not internal-signal based - good contrast.

idea: Large-scale reliability analysis of web agents across existing benchmarks, revealing performance variance under identical settings.
gap: Analysis framework only, not an environment/benchmark itself.

paper: GEM: Gaussian Embedding Modeling for OOD Detection in GUI Agents
idea: Models trajectory embeddings as Gaussians to detect out-of-distribution risk in GUI agents.
Identifies Repetition-Induced Termination and Goal Conflict as GUI agent failure modes.
gap: Descriptive taxonomy, not a detection method.

idea: Trajectory-conditioned diagnosis distinguishing software defects from agent-side execution misses.
Benchmarks trustworthiness of multimodal LLM agents in GUI environments across perception, reasoning, safety.
Benchmark + trajectory synthesis for GUI agent robustness.

paper: Beyond pass@1: A Reliability Science Framework for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
idea: Applies reliability-engineering concepts (MTBF, failure rate, reliability function) to long-horizon agents.
gap: Empirical grounding for treating 'reliability' as a measurable quantity, not just pass/fail.

paper: Language Models (Mostly) Know What They Know
idea: Models can evaluate whether their own statements are true and predict correctness in advance.

paper: Uncertainty-Based Abstention Improves Safety and Reduces Hallucinations
idea: Statistical + In-Dialogue Uncertainty thresholding drives abstention; improves correctness and catches ~50% hallucinations.

paper: Simple Probes can Catch Sleeper Agents
idea: Linear probing detects a backdoored model about to behave dangerously, even after it appears safe.
gap: Backdoor/deception setting, not task-failure prediction - your transfer target.

idea: Benchmarks reusing LLM internals for cheaper monitoring than full output-classifier passes.

paper: Harmful Intent as a Geometrically Recoverable Feature
idea: Linear direction for harmful intent, stable across 12 models and 4 architectures.
gap: TPR@1%FPR only 0.30 for a deployed classifier despite high AUROC.

paper: Why Safety Probes Catch Liars But Miss Fanatics
idea: Diff-in-means probing distinguishes deceptive vs. self-consistent hostile models.
gap: Probes miss agents that internally believe their own harmful reasoning.

idea: Trains LLMs to answer questions about their own activations in natural language.
Trains an LLM to self-report behaviors learned during fine-tuning; generalizes across fine-tuning methods.

paper: Detecting Multi-Agent Collusion Through Multi-Agent Interpretability
idea: Extends single-agent deception probing to multi-agent collusion detection, framed via AI Control.

paper: Reasoning Models Don't Always Say What They Think
idea: Reasoning models don't always verbalize true reasoning.
gap: Caveat for you: text/CoT-based trajectory monitoring has a ceiling - motivates activation-level signals.

idea: Training on the model's own reasoning about why an action is wrong generalizes better OOD than demonstration-matching.
Realistic training processes can accidentally produce misaligned models via reward hacking.

paper: Monitoring Computer Use via Hierarchical Summarization
idea: Anthropic's own approach to monitoring computer-use agent trajectories via hierarchical summarization.
gap: Summarization/text-based, not internal-state-based - direct prior art to position against for GUI/computer-use.

idea: Benchmark of evasive transcripts exploiting blind spots of frontier monitoring systems.
Argues catching-and-fixing misalignment is tractable even without solving alignment.
